# Org, Team & RBAC Module LLM Guide

Purpose: multi-tenant organizations, memberships, roles, and invitations for
Cloudflare Workers + D1. The B2B foundation other modules scope against.

Entry points:
- `src/index.ts` exports the safe public surface.
- `src/use-cases/` holds framework-neutral behavior: createOrganization,
  inviteMember, acceptInvitation, updateMemberRole, removeMember, authorize
  (+ resolvePermissions), listMembers, createRole.
- `src/authz.ts` is permission matching (wildcards: "*", "prefix.*").
- `src/ports/` defines RbacStore.
- `src/adapters/` contains D1 + memory stores.
- `migrations/0001_org_team_rbac.sql` owns organizations, memberships, roles, invitations.
- `module.json` is the machine-readable manifest.

Model:
- Platform module. New orgs seed owner/admin/member roles; the creator is owner.
- Lifecycle: invite (token) -> accept (single-use) -> membership; roles carry
  permission strings resolved at authorize time.

Correctness invariants (the point of the module — do not remove):
- Tenant isolation: a non-member resolves to [] permissions; membership keyed by (org,user).
- Permissions resolve from the role with wildcard matching, not flat role checks.
- Invitations are single-use and expire.
- The last owner cannot be removed or downgraded.

Agent rules:
- Keep use cases neutral; persistence behind RbacStore.
- Never make real I/O in tests; inject createMemoryRbacStore().
- Never read/write across orgs without an (orgId, userId) scope.
- Ask before changing migrations, permission logic, or production deploy.
